rows.description: Soluta varia.
rows.notes: Page title clearly centered at top with no amounts.

rows.description: Acct Dinner
rows.notes: Description legible as 'Acct Dinner'. Right-hand three figures read as '2' (pounds), '17' (shillings), '9' (pence). Slight ink blur on the pence but column alignment is clear.

rows.description: To Coques for fewel &c.
rows.notes: Description reads 'To Coques for fewel &c.' Pounds column looks like '26' though the leading digit could be a '2' with an unclear trailing mark; shillings and pence appear as '0 0' aligned under columns. Moderate ambiguity due to slightly faded ink.

rows.description: To Groves Admins?trs for fewel &c.
rows.notes: Text reads 'To Groves Admins[trs] for fewel &c.' but the amount area for this line is partly obscured by a vertical crease and the figures are not legible; left blank due to unreadability.

rows.description: To Tho. Clark Smith for a lock and 2 keys to ye common
rows.notes: Description clearly reads as given. Amount columns read '0' (pounds), '4' (shillings), '6' (pence). Pence digit has slight flourish but fits column alignment and is valid (<=11).

rows.description: Library
rows.notes: Single-word section header 'Library' clearly written and has no amounts.

rows.description: For Bickettrey books Nov. 30th 18? Bapt. 16?
rows.notes: Description partly abbreviated; dates uncertain but amounts read as '0' (pounds), '3' (shillings), '8' (pence). Slight uncertainty on the description dating digits; amount columns are legible.

rows.description: To mr Wyle for a table-cloth and two towells
rows.notes: Text clear. Amount columns read '0. 1. 4' with good alignment and clear digits.

rows.description: To mr Wyle for 2 doz: of napkins
rows.notes: Description legible; amounts read '0' pounds, '1' shilling, '11' pence. Pence '11' is clear though ink slightly darker on second '1'.

rows.description: For 2. vol. of my &d Clarendony Hist. presented to Dr Mans?o
rows.notes: Description is abbreviated and partly uncertain ('Clarendony' and recipient unclear). Amount columns appear '0.2.5' though the pounds column is blank/zero; moderate uncertainty from abbreviation in description.

rows.description: To Archbp of Armagh 1. vol. 1st 2d vol. 1. 4?
rows.notes: Handwriting for recipient and volumes is cramped; amount columns read '0' pounds, '2' shillings, '4' pence but the pence digit is a bit faint—entered with moderate uncertainty.

rows.description: For binding ye last vol. in past-board
rows.notes: Description clear. Amounts read '0' pounds, '2' shillings, '4' pence with good alignment under the three ruled columns.

rows.description: To Hilleard printer
rows.notes: Name reads 'Hilleard' (or similar) and amount '0.1.6' is clearly visible and valid.

rows.description: For 24 shings of velum for a book to register Benefact
rows.notes: Text reads 'For 24 shings of velum...' (shings = sheets?). Amount columns read '0' pounds, '6' shillings, '5' pence. Slight ambiguity on word 'shings' but numbers aligned.

rows.description: Matts for ye chapple
rows.notes: Entry 'Matts for ye chapple' clear. Amount appears as '0' pounds, '5' shillings, '0' pence; pence column blank but interpreted as zero due to alignment.

rows.description: Dinners Xmas day, New years day, Twelfth day
rows.notes: Description lists three days. Amount columns read '0.6.1' clearly aligned; text is legible.

rows.description: To Wright for cleaning ye upper room over ye Kitchen
rows.notes: Long description clearly legible. Amount columns distinctly read '14' (pounds), '19' (shillings), '9' (pence) with strong alignment — digits are dark and unambiguous.

rows.description: To England for bringing money for ye building
rows.notes: Text 'To England for bringing money for ye building' readable. Amount columns show a small '1' in pence column only; pounds and shillings appear blank/zero. Slight uncertainty because single pence with blanks could indicate omission.

rows.description: To John Baker for viver to ye kitchin and passage unknown
rows.notes: Description partially abbreviated ('viver' = viver?). Amount columns read '0.1.4'; the pence digit is faint but aligns with the pence column.

rows.description: To Coseny Ann. s. 14. 6. Mich. 3. 12. 0
rows.notes: This line appears to be part of a grouped set (bracketed) with complex internal numbers (Ann. s. 14.6 etc.). Final aligned amount reads '0' pounds, '10' shillings, '0' pence but interpretation of internal figures is uncertain. Moderate-low confidence due to complexity of entries and abbreviations.

rows.description: To Broadwater Ann. 6. 0. 6.
rows.notes: This is the second bracketed item; description and amount '0.6.0' read clearly though the interior punctuation and annotations are abbreviated.

rows.description: For ruling and binding ye book for Benefactors names to ye New-buildings
rows.notes: Long description legible. Amount '0.2.5' visible and well aligned, digits clear.

rows.description: To Dully for a case of dogs skin to ye book
rows.notes: Description likely 'To Dully for a case of dog-skin to ye book'. Amount shows '0.0.7' with the pence '7' clear; pounds/shillings blank/zero. Slight uncertainty on the proper name 'Dully'.

rows.description: To mr Sury for ye Frontispiece to ye s. book
rows.notes: Name reads 'mr Sury' (or 'Sury') and 'Frontispiece' clear. Amount '0.1.1' legible with good alignment.

rows.description: To mr Dubber for mending coll. plate
rows.notes: Name likely 'Dubber' and purpose 'mending coll. plate' clear. Amount columns read '0.1.6' distinctly.

rows.description: To mr Pain ye Brazier
rows.notes: Surname 'Pain' and occupation 'Brazier' readable. Amount '0.2.3' visible though faint; aligned under the three columns.

rows.description: For carriage of Books from Secker given by mr Long
rows.notes: Description legible. The rightmost figures appear to be '0.4.16' but pence '16' exceeds valid pence (must be 0–11). This indicates probable misalignment or extra digit; due to that inconsistency I left the pence as '16' as read but assigned low confidence and note that column alignment is likely off here — original likely '0.4.1 6s' or similar. Needs re-examination of column boundaries.

rows.description: To mr Rivon painter for ye chapple
rows.notes: Entry reads 'mr Rivon painter for ye chapple'. Right-hand shows '00 17' across two rightmost columns in the image; pence '17' would be invalid (>=12), indicating misplacement of digits across columns. Column alignment is unclear here so reading is unreliable; very low confidence.

rows.description: To mr Remel for reading prayers; and for what he laid out for mr Longs books
rows.notes: Long description legible. Amount appears as '0.14.0' (14 shillings). Shillings within 0–19 and alignment is reasonable; pence blank interpreted as zero.

rows.description: To Wright for cleaning ye back yard; and for cleaning ye yard behind ye Lodgings 2s
rows.notes: Description shows two cleaning items; final aligned amount reads '0.3.0'. The embedded '2s' in the text may be a note; used the final aligned figure for extraction.

rows.description: To Day for chains and work att mr Longs booky
rows.notes: Text 'To Day for chains and work...' readable but the monetary columns are faint or possibly absent on this line; left as zero/blank with low confidence because amounts may be off-page or missing.

rows.description: To West for binding some of mr Longs books
rows.notes: Description clear. The rightmost section near this line shows '02 5 8' but '8' in pence column could be a pence or part of adjacent ink; also the pence here might be actually '8d' which is valid but the image shows an extra flourish. Column alignment is slightly off and there is ink crowding; very low confidence. (If interpreted strictly, amount = £0 2s 5 8d is impossible; I preserved digits as seen but flagged uncertainty.)

rows.description: To mr Sury for writing ye names of Benefactrs
rows.notes: Description readable. The rightmost shows '00 10 1' in the image overlapping adjacent lines; the intended amount might be 0s10d or 0s10s1d; due to ambiguity I set pence to 10 and left pounds/shillings blank; low confidence.

rows.description: To Counsell abt ye title of ye Advowson of Somerford
rows.notes: Description legible with abbreviations. Right-hand amounts read as '0.3.1' though the pence digit is faint; moderate confidence.

rows.description: For wine Octobr 5th
rows.notes: Short description and date written. Amount '0.2.3' is clearly aligned and legible.

rows.description: To mr Jackson for setting the College Arms
rows.notes: Text reads 'To mr Jackson for setting the Collge Arms'. Amount columns show '0.1.4' distinctly.

rows.description: on the books in both Librarys
rows.notes: This is a continuation line 'on the books in both Librarys'. Rightmost reads '0.2.15' but 15 pence is invalid; this indicates digits likely misaligned between shillings and pence. Very low confidence because column boundaries appear shifted here.

rows.description: To him for the plate
rows.notes: Short description 'To him for the plate'. Final pence '5' visible in rightmost column; pounds and shillings appear blank/zero. Moderate confidence due to some ink bleed at line end.

rows.description: Sum
rows.notes: Final summary at bottom right clearly reads 'Sum' with totals '111' (pounds), '17' (shillings), '5' (pence). These digits are bold and aligned to the three monetary columns; high confidence.
